runTitle: Vercel Playwright MCP QA - Reporter and Analyst Functional Quality Check
coveredSurfaces: Vercel production AI sidebar preserved guest-authenticated session from dashboard
Reporter tab opens from AI sidebar
Reporter empty state and first-report CTA render
Reporter generates Redis memory overload incident report through /api/ai/incident-report 200
Reporter result identifies cache-redis-dc1-01 as impacted server with 80% confidence root-cause summary
Reporter state remains present after switching to Analyst and back
Analyst tab opens from AI sidebar
Analyst full-system analysis executes 18 /api/ai/intelligent-monitoring requests with 200 responses
Analyst result renders 17 normal, 1 caution, 0 risk, plus cache-redis-dc1-01 detail with current state, one-hour prediction, and AI insight
Analyst NaN prediction regression was not observed
skippedSurfaces: Broad landing/privacy/modal sweep skipped; scope limited to Reporter and Analyst AI advanced surfaces
OAuth provider end-to-end login skipped; existing guest session was sufficient for this targeted QA
Cloud Run admin monitoring/traces skipped; Vercel AI proxy paths covered
Reporter detail modal deep controls were not expanded; generated report card and summary were validated

expertAssessments.rationale: Reporter and Analyst both complete on production, but Analyst trend result formatting and issue ranking still need non-blocking polish.
expertAssessments.nextAction: Clarify Analyst trend target values and suppress or explain low-priority issue rows such as CPU 24%.

expertAssessments.rationale: Playwright MCP exercised Reporter generation, Analyst full-system analysis, state switching, network status, and console cleanliness.
Vercel usage remained normal and all tested AI proxy calls returned 200.
Core counts and warning server identification are coherent, but the Analyst main-issues ranking includes a CPU 24% row that needs clearer context or filtering.
expertAssessments.nextAction: Improve Analyst issue ranking/formatting for low-utilization outliers and missing prediction target display.

usageChecks.summary: npm run check:usage:vercel passed after Reporter/Analyst MCP QA; current billing period effective=17.7346 USD, billed=0.0000 USD, chargeCount=15834.

artifacts.label: Reporter and Analyst MCP QA summary

completedImprovements.title: Reporter and Analyst production MCP functional check on v8.11.36
completedImprovements.evidence: Reporter generated a Redis memory incident report; Analyst completed 18-server full-system analysis with no console errors and no NaN prediction regression.

wontFixImprovements.title: Analyst trend target formatting and issue ranking need polish
wontFixImprovements.overengineeringScope: Functional AI paths are green; this is a result-clarity improvement, not a release blocker.
wontFixImprovements.evidence: Analyst result showed trend rows like `CPU 69% → --` and listed `storage-nfs-dc1-02 - CPU 24%` under main issues without enough context.
wontFixImprovements.note: Replace missing target values with explicit labels or numeric projections, and either filter or explain low-priority issue rows.
wontFixImprovements.policyNote: 포트폴리오 운영성 우선 규칙: 비차단 항목은 과도한 개선을 방지하기 위해 WONT-FIX 처리합니다.

notes: Reporter generated `Redis 서버 메모리 과부하 경고` with impacted server cache-redis-dc1-01 and confidence 80%.
Analyst full-system analysis completed for 18 servers and no NaN prediction text was observed.
Quality judgment: AI Chat and Reporter are improved/acceptable; Analyst is functionally stable but needs clarity polish.
mode audit: advanced Reporter/Analyst surfaces used their dedicated AI proxy paths rather than chat-only evaluation.
